metrics.name: BoJ
metrics.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

simulation_result.question: Will the Bank of Japan raise its short-term policy interest rate at the June 15–16, 2026 Monetary Policy Meeting?
limitations: forecast decomposition — grounded prior 0.875 (counted n=3); simulation-conditional None (resolved mass 0.00); headline 0.875 via prior_only_simulation_unavailable. Prior and simulation are reported separately and never blended by a fixed rule.
unresolved [unresolved_unknown_state]: 0.936 — explicit other_unknown_state mass; feasible-action bounds computed, never assigned prior/0.5
unresolved [unresolved_future_decision]: 0.064 — advance simulated time to the deadline; the obligation reopens the decision

no leakage-audited prior↔simulation reliability combiner is fitted — the headline is the simulation-conditional forecast; the grounded prior is reported separately; this is a stated reason Lean V2 must not become the default yet

blueprint.causal_thesis: The BOJ Policy Board votes on a proposal to raise the short-term policy rate from 0.75% to 1.0%. The outcome hinges on whether the three dissenting members from April (Nakagawa, Takata, Tamura) gain one more vote, or whether a majority coalesces to keep rates unchanged. Market expectations and econo

japan_economic_regime.claim: Japan is in a moderate inflation and growth regime, with the BOJ gradually normalizing policy after years of ultra-loose stance.

provenance.definition: Japan is in a moderate inflation and growth regime, with the BOJ gradually normalizing policy after years of ultra-loose stance.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: BOJ raised rate from 0.0-0.1% to 0.25% in July 2024, first hike in 17 years, amid rising inflation and wage growth.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.25%.

cases.description: BOJ raised rate from 0.25% to 0.5% in January 2025, citing sustained inflation above target and positive wage cycle.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.5%.

cases.description: BOJ raised rate from 0.5% to 0.75% in March 2025, continuing normalization.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.75%.

consensus_pressure_for_hike.claim: Market and internal pressure for a June hike is high, with dissenting members advocating for 1.0%.

provenance.definition: Market and internal pressure for a June hike is high, with dissenting members advocating for 1.0%.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: At April 2026 meeting, three members proposed raising rate to 1.0%, but were defeated 6-3.
cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

leadership_stance.claim: Governor Ueda has signaled gradual normalization but has not committed to a June hike, emphasizing data dependence.

provenance.definition: Governor Ueda has signaled gradual normalization but has not committed to a June hike, emphasizing data dependence.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: Ueda's press conference after April 2026 meeting emphasized need for more data on wages and consumption.
cases.basis_quote: Governor Ueda stated that the Bank needs to scrutinize more data on wage developments and consumption before adjusting policy.

provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any vote where the member proposes a rate increase and votes against the hold decision.
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Votes to hold or cut; abstentions.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 1 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.basis_quote: Three dissenting members — Nakagawa Junko, Takata Hajime, and Tamura Naoki — proposed raising the rate to 1.0%, but their proposals were defeated.

cases.basis_quote: The Policy Board voted 6-3 to keep the rate unchanged.

outcome_reference_class.quantity: BOJ raises short-term policy interest rate at June 2026 meeting

provenance.definition: The BOJ announces a guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate higher than 0.75% (i.e., at least 1.0%) in the official Statement on Monetary Policy after the June 15-16, 2026 meeting.
provenance.inclusion_criteria: Any announced rate above 0.75% (e.g., 1.0% or higher).
provenance.exclusion_criteria: Rate remains at 0.75% or lower; meeting cancelled; no decision.
provenance.level_fallback_reason: all levels sparse (total 3 usable case(s)); rate is wide by construction

cases.description: BOJ raised rate from 0.5% to 0.75% at March 2025 meeting.
cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.75%.

cases.basis_quote: The Bank decided to raise the guideline for the uncollateralized overnight call rate to around 0.5%.

boj_policy_board.consequence_of_nonparticipation: Non-participating members do not affect quorum if majority present; their absence reduces total votes but decision still valid.

weight_invariant.rule: every weight traces to a counted reference class or is explicit unknown mass; no qualitative label is mapped to a number

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda privately believes that the available economic data by mid-June is insufficient to confirm a sustainable 2% inflation target, and that premature tightening could derail the fragile recovery.
goals: Maintain credibility of inflation targeting framework
Avoid policy mistake that reverses recovery
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Market expectations for a hike are high, but Ueda prioritizes data confirmation over market timing.
supporting_evidence_ids: Oxford Economics Nagai's view on insufficient data
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not explicitly pre-committed to June hike
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote to hold the rate at 0.75%, arguing that more data is needed.
assumptions: Inflation data for April-May will be mixed
Wage negotiations show only modest gains
transition_triggers: Stronger-than-expected CPI or wage data before June 15

ueda_kazuo.claim: Ueda privately concludes that the risk of inflation becoming entrenched outweighs the risk of a slowdown, and that a hike to 1.0% is necessary to maintain policy credibility and prevent yen weakness.
beliefs: Labor market tightness supports wage-price spiral
goals: Maintain BOJ independence from political pressure
ueda_kazuo.pressures: Strong market pricing for hike, internal dissent from three members, and yen weakness.
supporting_evidence_ids: Three dissenting votes for 1.0% in April
historical_case_refs: BOJ 2006-2007 rate hikes to prevent bubble
Ueda's academic work on preemptive policy
distinguishing_observations: Ueda has not ruled out June hike
He acknowledged upside inflation risks in recent press conference
ueda_kazuo.action_if_state: Vote to raise the rate to 1.0%, leading a majority coalition with the three dissenters.
assumptions: April-May inflation data will be strong

nakagawa_junko.claim: Nakagawa remains convinced that the BOJ must raise rates to 1.0% to combat inflation and normalize policy, and she will again propose a hike regardless of Ueda's stance.
beliefs: Inflation is above target and persistent
goals: Achieve 1.0% rate as soon as possible
nakagawa_junko.pressures: Internal consistency and market expectations; she is a known hawk.
distinguishing_observations: She has never voted to hold when she proposed a hike
She publicly argues inflation is not transitory
nakagawa_junko.action_if_state: Propose a 25bp hike to 1.0% and vote in favor; if Ueda opposes, she will dissent again.
assumptions: Inflation data will support her view
No new shock that changes her mind

takata_hajime.claim: Takata favors a hike to 1.0% but is open to delaying if new data shows weakness; he will vote with Nakagawa unless Ueda provides a compelling data-based argument to wait.
commitments: Emphasized data dependence in past comments
takata_hajime.pressures: Balancing hawkish reputation with flexibility; market expectations.
supporting_evidence_ids: His past statements on need for normalization
contradicting_evidence_ids: His occasional dovish comments on global risks
historical_case_refs: Takata's voting pattern shows occasional shifts
distinguishing_observations: He is less rigid than Nakagawa
He has voted with majority in past when data was weak
takata_hajime.action_if_state: Vote for a hike to 1.0% if data supports; otherwise, may vote to hold if Ueda presents strong evidence.

tamura_naoki.claim: Tamura is firmly convinced that a hike to 1.0% is necessary and will not be swayed by Ueda's caution; he will vote for a hike and dissent if needed.
tamura_naoki.pressures: Internal consistency; he is a known hawk.
supporting_evidence_ids: His public statements on inflation risks
distinguishing_observations: He has been the most hawkish member
tamura_naoki.action_if_state: Propose and vote for a 25bp hike to 1.0%; dissent if majority holds.

other_members.claim: The other five members (who voted to hold in April) remain cautious, believing that more data is needed and that Ueda's leadership should be followed; they will vote to hold unless Ueda signals a hike.
other_members.pressures: Groupthink and deference to Ueda; market expectations are secondary.
distinguishing_observations: They have not publicly advocated for a hike
other_members.action_if_state: Vote to hold at 0.75% if Ueda votes hold; may switch if Ueda signals a hike.

other_members.claim: The other five members become convinced by strong data and Ueda's potential shift that a hike is necessary; they will vote for a hike to 1.0%.
beliefs: BOJ must act to maintain credibility
commitments: Previously voted hold but open to data
other_members.pressures: Market expectations, yen depreciation, and potential leadership shift.
distinguishing_observations: They are not ideologically committed to hold
other_members.action_if_state: Vote for a 25bp hike to 1.0% if Ueda leads or data strongly supports.

checks.note: all terminal-writing effects are known mechanical kinds
at least one resolving path exists

deliberations.trigger: internal_conflict_named: Whether the recent inflation stickiness is transitory or persistent, and how global financial conditions will evolve.
deliberations.note: The inflation stickiness is concerning, but global risks—especially from US trade policy and financial volatility—are too uncertain to justify a hike now. Nakagawa and Tamura would likely support wait

coalescer.randomness_rule: content-addressed (state/context-seeded) draws — equal keys share the future transition law by construction; independent streams carry a tag that refuses the merge

engine_primary.weight_source: counted_reference_class_posteriors (no qualitative label is mapped to a number anywhere in this engine)

skipped_reasons: no deterministic trigger fired: primary stable, no verified evidence conflict, no reversal-capable disputed assumption (imagination alone is not a trigger)

replicate_policy.reason: status 'unresolved' is not scoreable — replicate would repeat the same missing mechanism, not test execution uncertainty

treatments.unresolved_unknown_state: explicit other_unknown_state mass; feasible-action bounds computed, never assigned prior/0.5
treatments.unresolved_future_decision: advance simulated time to the deadline; the obligation reopens the decision
